# Copilot code review — devantler-tech/reusable-workflows

This repo is a library of **reusable `workflow_call` GitHub workflows** (`.github/workflows/<name>.yaml`)
consumed by CI/CD across every devantler-tech product. Sibling to
[actions](https://github.com/devantler-tech/actions) (composite actions, not workflows). Canonical
conventions live in [`AGENTS.md`](../AGENTS.md); this is the concise review checklist — flag
violations of the rules below.

## Always (every PR)

- **Conventional-Commit PR title** (`feat:`/`fix:`/`docs:`/`ci:`/`build:`/`chore:`/`refactor:`/`test:`).
  semantic-release cuts releases from the squash-merge title, so a non-conventional or bracket-prefixed
  title is a defect. The workflows **are** this repo's product: `ci:`/`build:`/`refactor:` ship to
  consumers as a **patch**, `feat:` as minor, a `!` or `BREAKING CHANGE:` as **major** — flag a title
  whose type understates the actual change (e.g. a behaviour change typed `chore:`/`docs:`, which would
  ship no release).
- **Root-cause fixes only.** Flag anything that masks a failure: `continue-on-error` or a deleted
  `[Test]` job to dodge red CI, weakened assertions, `--no-verify`, a silenced/skipped `zizmor` or
  linter check.
- **Behaviour / input / secret / output change ⇒ docs updated in the same PR.** Update the workflow's
  entry in the repo `README.md` (usage, inputs, secrets, outputs); a missing doc update is a finding.
- **No secrets in plaintext** — flag committed tokens/keys, or `${{ secrets.* }}` echoed into logs.
- PRs from the Daily AI Assistant carry a `> 🤖 Generated by the Daily AI Assistant` line — expected,
  not a finding.

## Reusable workflows

- **Blast radius first:** every workflow has many consumer repos, so changes must be **additive and
  backward-compatible**. Flag a renamed/removed/retyped `input`, `secret`, or `output` unless it's
  called out as a deliberate breaking change (`!`/`BREAKING CHANGE:`); prefer a defaulted addition.
- Every reusable workflow uses the **`workflow_call`** trigger. Workflows used as org-level repository
  **rulesets** also need `pull_request:` **and** `merge_group:` triggers — flag a ruleset workflow
  missing either.
- **Test coverage is part of done:** each reusable workflow is exercised by a `test-<workflow-name>`
  job **inside [`ci.yaml`](workflows/ci.yaml)** (there are no standalone `test-*.yaml` files),
  calling it with safe/dry-run params + `.github/fixtures/`. A new/changed workflow without its `[Test]`
  job is a coverage gap. The job must be wired into `ci-required-checks` in **two** places — the
  `needs:` list **and** the `job-results` input of its `aggregate-job-checks` step; a job in `needs:`
  but absent from `job-results` has its failure **silently ignored** (flag this).

## CI & security

- **Pin third-party actions** (non-`actions/*`, non-`github/*`, non-`devantler-tech/*`) to a full commit
  SHA with a `# <version>` comment — enforced by `zizmor.yml`; flag unpinned or tag-pinned refs.
- Every job's **first step** is `step-security/harden-runner`. Set **`permissions: {}` at the workflow
  top level** and grant least-privilege per job. Set **`persist-credentials: false`** on
  `actions/checkout` unless the job must push.
- Operations that trigger other workflows / bypass branch protection use a **GitHub App token**
  (`actions/create-github-app-token` with `APP_ID` var + `APP_PRIVATE_KEY` secret), not `GITHUB_TOKEN`.
- Never weaken a security control (harden-runner, SHA pin, `persist-credentials: false`, `permissions`)
  to make a check pass.

Copilot code review reads this file + `.github/instructions/**/*.instructions.md` at **≤4000 chars**
and does **not** read `AGENTS.md` — when unsure, defer to [`AGENTS.md`](../AGENTS.md).
